title: The history of the British wool industry 英国羊毛产业的历史

passages.title: The history of the British wool industry 英国羊毛产业的历史
intro: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13.

groups.title: Questions 1–5
raw_lines: Do the following statements agree with the information in Reading Passage 1?
1 The process of making cloth from wool was introduced to Britain by the Romans.
TRUE FALSE NOT GIVEN
2 In the twelfth century, exporting woollen cloth was less profitable than exporting raw wool.
TRUE FALSE NOT GIVEN
3 Rulers had a financial interest in the success of the wool industry.
TRUE FALSE NOT GIVEN
4 An outbreak of bubonic plague led to a sharp fall in sheep numbers.
TRUE FALSE NOT GIVEN
5 Worsted cloth was cheaper to produce than other types of woollen fabric.
TRUE FALSE NOT GIVEN
instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information in Reading Passage 1?
groups.source_html:
Questions 1–5

Do the following statements agree with the information in Reading Passage 1?

1 The process of making cloth from wool was introduced to Britain by the Romans.

2 In the twelfth century, exporting woollen cloth was less profitable than exporting raw wool.

3 Rulers had a financial interest in the success of the wool industry.

4 An outbreak of bubonic plague led to a sharp fall in sheep numbers.

5 Worsted cloth was cheaper to produce than other types of woollen fabric.

groups.title: Questions 6–13
raw_lines: Complete the notes below. Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Woollen cloth manufacture
Growing importance of the cloth industry:
16th century: skilled 6 emigrated to England
end 17th century: majority of English 7 were wool products
18th century: production of worsted cloth increased in Yorkshire – growth of five key manufacturing 8
1750–1850: new machinery was developed – initially for the production of 9
1812: protests resulted in the 10 of machinery
19th century: in Yorkshire mechanisation increased, aided by the availability of cheap 11
Growth of specialisation:
Scotland – specialised in 12
West Country – specialised in 13

Questions 6–13

Complete the notes below. Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Woollen cloth manufacture

Growing importance of the cloth industry:

  • 16th century: skilled 6 emigrated to England
  • end 17th century: majority of English 7 were wool products
  • 18th century: production of worsted cloth increased in Yorkshire – growth of five key manufacturing 8
  • 1750–1850: new machinery was developed – initially for the production of 9
  • 1812: protests resulted in the 10 of machinery
  • 19th century: in Yorkshire mechanisation increased, aided by the availability of cheap 11

Growth of specialisation:

  • Scotland – specialised in 12
  • West Country – specialised in 13
